1.5: ), as on the Gentiles who were then called from all the nations of the world. ― My name , etc. See, Daniel ,
1.26: Kijoum was a Babylonian god corresponding to the planet Saturn.
1.24: . The House of Israel ; the assemblies of the people of Israel.
1.33: ). Since the 14th e century, Thécué was abandoned and destroyed.
1.15: Since only a very small number of Israelites returned from captivity, and their descendants were taken from their country by the Romans, it seems that this is a promise of the spiritual blessings that God reserves for the children of Israel for the time of their conversion to Christianity.
1.12: the rest of Edom , rival and enemy of the house of Jacob, may represent the very carnal Jews, from among whom God, by his grace, saved precious remnants whom he reunited with his people, that is, with his Church, by pouring out the gift of faith upon them (see Romans ,
1.3: , the Lord refuses to forgive again. — Between these last two, in ch.
1.16: ), which had a beginning of fulfillment through Cyrus, but which were not fully accomplished until Jesus Christ.
1.11: I will point out , etc. The apostle Saint James quotes these words (see Acts of the Apostles ,
1.14: The sin of Samaria ; the false god Baal. ― Your God, O Dan . One of the golden calves had been placed in Dan, a city located near the sources of the Jordan. Dan, Bersabée . Judges
1.22: . ― The ivory houses , in which there were ornaments and ivory inlays.
1.27: ), while they only use it for their own pleasure.
1.19: The snake hidden in a hole in the wall. Snakes are numerous in Palestine and some are very venomous.
1.42: . "One could please God with a poor but sincere worship" Osty, 1952.
1.17: your land will be divided by the meter , shared by the enemy.
1.41: . I will put , etc.; I will force you all to wear sackcloth and shave your heads. This was the custom during mourning.

2.1: This is what the Lord says: Because of three sins of Moab, and even four, I will not revoke him. Because he burned the bones of the king of Edom until they were charred,
2.2: I will send fire upon Moab and it will devour the palaces of Carioth, and Moab will die amidst tumult, war cries, and the sound of the trumpet.
2.3: I will exterminate the Judge from his bosom and I will slaughter with him all his princes, says the Lord.
2.4: Thus says the Lord: For three sins of Judah, and indeed for four, I will not revoke it. Because they have rejected the law of the Lord and have not kept his ordinances, and because their false idols have led them astray, the idols their ancestors followed,
2.5: I will send fire upon Judah, and it will devour the palaces of Jerusalem.
2.6: Thus says the Lord: For three sins of Israel, and even for four, I will not revoke it, because they sell the righteous for silver and the needy for a pair of sandals,
2.7: because they long to see the dust of the earth on the heads of the wretched and they make the path of the lowly bend, because the son and his father go to the same girl, to profane my holy name,
2.8: because they lie on garments received as pledges, beside all the altars, and they drink the wine of those they have fined, in the house of their God.
2.9: And yet I had destroyed before them the Amorite, whose height was like that of the cedars and whose strength was like the oaks; I had destroyed his fruit above and his roots below.
2.10: And yet I brought you up from the land of Egypt and led you forty years in the desert, to put you in possession of the land of the Amorites.
2.11: I raised up prophets from among your sons and Nazirites from among your young men, is it not so, O children of Israel? declares the Lord.
2.12: But you made the Nazirites drink wine and you gave this order to the prophets: «Do not prophesy.».
2.13: Behold, I will trample you down, as a cart tramples the earth when it is full of sheaves.
2.14: The swift man will fail to escape, the vigorous man will find no strength, and the valiant man will not save his life.
2.15: And he who wields the bow will not stand, and the swift-footed man will not escape, and the horseman will not save his life.
2.16: And the bravest of the brave will flee naked on that day, declares the Lord.

3.1: Listen to this word that the Lord has spoken concerning you, children of Israel, «concerning all the family that I brought up out of the land of Egypt,» in these words:
3.2: I have known no one but you among all the families of the earth, therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.
3.3: Do two men walk together without having agreed?
3.4: Does the lion roar in the forest, without having caught anything? Does the lion cub make its voice resound from the depths of its den, without having taken anything?
3.5: Does the sparrow fall into the net on the ground, without any bait for it? Does the net rise from the ground without it having caught anything?
3.6: When a trumpet is sounded in a city, do not the people tremble? When disaster strikes a city, is not the Lord responsible?
3.7: For the Lord God does nothing without revealing his secret to his servants, the prophets.
3.8: The lion has roared: who will not fear? The Lord God has spoken: who will not prophesy?
3.9: Proclaim this word upon the palaces in Azotus and upon the palaces in the land of Egypt and say: Assemble yourselves upon the mountains of Samaria and see what great disorder is in the midst of her and what violence within her.
3.10: And they do not know how to act with justice, declares the Lord, they who store up violence and plunder in their palaces.
3.11: Therefore this is what the Lord God says: Behold, the enemy surrounds the land, he will strip you of your strength and your palaces will be plundered.
3.12: Thus says the Lord: As a shepherd rescues two hamstrings or a piece of an ear from the mouth of a lion, so shall the children of Israel be saved, who sit in Samaria, in the corner of a couch, on cushions of Damascus.
3.13: Listen and bear witness to this in the house of Jacob, declares the Lord God, the God of hosts:
3.14: On the day I avenge on him the transgressions of Israel, I will also avenge the altars of Bethel; the horns of the altar shall be broken and fall to the ground.
3.15: I will strike the winter house, along with the summer house; the ivory houses will be devastated and many houses will be destroyed, declares the Lord.

4.1: Listen to this word, you cows of Bashan, who are on the mountain of Samaria, you who oppress the weak and trample the poor, you who say to your husbands, "Bring us something to drink."«
4.2: The Lord God has sworn by his holiness: Behold, the days will come upon you when you will be taken away with hooks and your offspring with harpoons.
4.3: You will go out through the breaches, each one straight ahead, and you will be thrown into Armon, declares the Lord.
4.4: Come to Bethel and sin, to Gilgal and multiply your sins. Bring your sacrifices every morning and your tithes every three days.
4.5: Burn an unleavened offering of praise, announce voluntary contributions, and proclaim them. For this is what you love, children of Israel, declares the Lord God.
4.6: Therefore I have given you clear teeth in all your cities and a lack of bread in all your dwellings, and you have not returned to me, declares the Lord.
4.7: So I withheld the rain from you, when there were still three months before the harvest, I made it rain on one city and I did not make it rain on another city, one region was watered by the rain and another region, on which it did not rain, dried up.
4.8: Two or three cities ran to another city to drink water, but they did not quench their thirst, and you did not return to me, declares the Lord.
4.9: I struck you with blight and mildew, your many gardens, your vineyards, your fig trees and your olive trees; the locust devoured them, and you did not return to me, declares the Lord.
4.10: I sent plague among you, in the manner of Egypt, I killed your young men with the sword, while your horses were captured, I made the stench rise from your camp and it reached your nostrils and you did not return to me, oracle of the Lord.
4.11: I have caused upheaval in you, as God upheaved Sodom and Gomorrah, and you were like a burning stick plucked from the fire, and you did not return to me, declares the Lord.
4.12: Therefore, this is what I will do to you, Israel. Since I am going to do this to you, prepare to meet your God, Israel.
4.13: For it is he who formed the mountains and created the wind, who reveals to man his thoughts, who turns the dawn into darkness and treads upon the heights of the earth. The Lord, the God of hosts, is his name.

5.1: Listen to this word that I speak over you, a lamentation, O house of Israel:
5.2: She has fallen, she will not rise again, the virgin of Israel. She is overthrown on her land, no one will raise her up.
5.3: For thus says the Lord: the city which went out to war with a thousand warriors shall keep a hundred, and the one which went out to war with a hundred shall keep ten, for the house of Israel.
5.4: For thus says the Lord to the house of Israel: Seek me and live.
5.5: Do not seek Bethel, do not come to Galgala, do not cross over to Beersheba. For Galgala will be taken captive and Bethel will become nothing.
5.6: Seek the Lord and live, lest he break out like a fire upon the house of Joseph and consume it, with no one to quench it in Bethel.
5.7: They turn the law into absinthe and throw justice to the ground.
5.8: He made the Pleiades and Orion. He turns darkness into dawn and day into deep night. He summons the waters of the sea and pours them out over the face of the earth. The Lord is his name.
5.9: He brings ruin upon the mighty, and ruin falls upon the strong city.
5.10: They hate at the Gate the one who censors and the one who speaks with integrity; they abhor him.
5.11: That is why, because you trample the poor underfoot and take from them a tribute of grain, you have built houses of hewn stone and you will not live in them, you have planted excellent vineyards and you will not drink their wine.
5.12: For I know that your crimes are many, that your sins are great, you who oppress the righteous, who take bribes and who deny justice to the poor at the Gate.
5.13: That is why the wise man keeps silent at this time, because it is an evil time.
5.14: Seek good and not evil, so that you may live and that the Lord God of hosts may be with you, as you say.
5.15: Hate evil and love good and restore justice to the Gate: perhaps the Lord, the God of hosts, will have mercy on the remnant of Joseph.
5.16: Therefore, this is what the Lord, the God of hosts, says: In every square there will be lamentation, in every street a cry of “Alas! Alas!” The farmer will be called to mourning and lamentation, along with those who know how to wail.
5.17: In all the vineyards there will be lamentation, for I will pass through the midst of you, says the Lord.
5.18: Woe to those who long for the day of the Lord! What will the day of the Lord be like for you? It will be darkness and not light.
5.19: Like a man who flees from a lion and the bear comes to meet him, he enters his house, puts his hand against the wall and the snake bites him.
5.20: Is not the day of the Lord darkness, and not light, but gloom without any brightness?
5.21: I hate, I despise your festivals, I have no taste for your gatherings.
5.22: If you offer me your burnt offerings and your grain offerings, I take no pleasure in them, and your sacrifices of fattened calves, I do not look upon them.
5.23: Take away from me the noise of your songs, and I will not hear the sound of your harps.
5.24: But let judgment flow like water and justice like a never-ending stream.
5.25: Have you offered me sacrifices and offerings in the wilderness for forty years, O house of Israel?
5.26: You carried the tent of your king and Kijoum, your idols, the star of your god, which you made for yourselves.
5.27: I will deport you beyond Damascus, says the Lord. God of hosts is his name.

6.1: Woe to those who live peacefully in Zion and securely on the mountain of Samaria, the noblest of the first of the peoples, to whom the house of Israel goes.
6.2: Go to Calneh and see. From there go to Hamath the great, down to Geth of the Philistines. Are these cities more prosperous than these kingdoms, and is their territory more extensive than yours?
6.3: You are putting off the day of misfortune and bringing near the reign of violence.
6.4: They lie on ivory beds and stretch out on their couches, they eat the lambs from the flock and the calves fattened in the stable.
6.5: They frolic to the sound of the harp, like David, they invented musical instruments.
6.6: They drink wine from large cups, they anoint themselves with the finest oils. And they are not sick with Joseph's wound.
6.7: That is why they will go into exile, at the head of the captives, and the cries of joy of the voluptuaries will disappear.
6.8: The Lord God has sworn by himself, declares the Lord, the God of hosts: I abhor the pride of Jacob and I hate his palaces. I will give up the city and everything in it.
6.9: And if ten men remain in a single house, they will die.
6.10: A relative with the one who burns the corpses shall remove the dead man and carry the bones out of the house, and one shall say to the one who is at the back of the house, "Are there any more with you?" He shall answer, "No one," and the first shall say, "Silence," for the name of the Lord must not be spoken.
6.11: For behold, the Lord commands, and he brings down the great house to ruins and the little house to rubble.
6.12: Do horses run on the rock, or do they plow with oxen, that you have changed justice into poison and the fruit of righteousness into wormwood?
6.13: You rejoice in what is nothing, saying, "Did we not acquire power by our own strength?"«
6.14: For behold, I am raising up against you, house of Israel, declares the Lord, the God of hosts, a nation which will oppress you from the entrance of Hamath to the stream of the desert.

7.1: This is how the Lord God showed me, and behold, he was forming locusts, at the very moment when the grass began to grow again, after the first cutting, that which is reserved for the king.
7.2: When they had finished devouring the grass of the earth, I said, «Lord God, please forgive me. How can Jacob endure it? He is so small.»
7.3: And the Lord relented about this: «It shall not be,» said the Lord.
7.4: Thus the Lord God showed me, and behold, the Lord God came to punish with fire, and the fire devoured the great abyss and it devoured the portion of the Lord.
7.5: And I said, «Lord God, please stop. How can Jacob stand? For he is small.»
7.6: The Lord relented: «This too shall not be,» said the Lord God.
7.7: Thus the Lord showed me, and behold, the Lord was standing on a vertical wall, and he had a plumb line in his hand.
7.8: And the Lord said to me, «What do you see, Amos?» I answered, «A plumb line,» and the Lord said to me, «Behold, I am setting the plumb line in the midst of my people Israel; I will no longer forgive them.
7.9: The high places of Isaac will be devastated and the sanctuaries of Israel will be destroyed, and I will rise up against the house of Jeroboam with the sword.»
7.10: And Amaziah, priest of Bethel, sent word to Jeroboam, king of Israel: «Amos is conspiring against you in the midst of the house of Israel; the land can no longer bear all his words.”
7.11: For Amos spoke thus: Jeroboam shall die by the sword, and Israel shall be taken captive from its land.»
7.12: And Amasias said to Amos, «Seer, go away, flee to the land of Judah and eat your bread there, and there you will prophesy.
7.13: But do not continue to prophesy at Bethel, for it is the king’s sanctuary and a royal residence.».
7.14: Amos answered and said to Amaziah, «I am not a prophet, I am not the son of a prophet, I am a herdsman and I tend sycamore trees.
7.15: And the Lord took me from behind the flock and the Lord said to me: Go, prophesy to my people Israel.
7.16: And now, listen to the word of the Lord: You say: You shall not prophesy against Israel and you shall not speak against the house of Isaac.
7.17: Therefore this is what the Lord says: »Your wife will become a prostitute in the city, your sons and daughters will fall by the sword, your land will be divided up by the measuring line, and you will die in an unclean land, and Israel will be taken captive from its land.”

8.1: Thus the Lord God showed me, and behold, a basket of ripe fruit.
8.2: And he said, «What do you see, Amos?» I replied, «A basket of ripe fruit.» And the Lord said to me, «The end has come for my people Israel; I will no longer forgive them.”.
8.3: The songs of the palace will be howls on that day, declares the Lord God. There will be many corpses. Everywhere they will be thrown down in silence.»
8.4: Listen to this, you who devour the poor and who would like to wipe the humble out of the country
8.5: saying, "When will the new moon be over, so that we may sell wheat, and the Sabbath, so that we may open our grain stores, making the ephah smaller and the shekel bigger, falsifying the scales to deceive?"
8.6: Shall we buy the wretched and the poor for money because of a pair of sandals, and sell the chaff of the wheat?»
8.7: The Lord swore by Jacob's pride: I will not forget all their deeds until the end.
8.8: Because of this, will not the earth tremble and all its inhabitants mourn? It will rise up entirely like the Nile, it will surge and sink, like the Nile of Egypt.
8.9: On that day, declares the Lord God, I will make the sun set at midday and I will cover the earth with darkness on a clear day.
8.10: I will turn your celebrations into mourning and all your songs of joy into lamentations, I will put sackcloth on every loins and I will make every head bald, I will make the land mourn for an only son and its end will be like a bitter day.
8.11: “The days are coming,” declares the Lord God, “when I will send a hunger on the earth—not a hunger for bread, nor a thirst for water, but a hunger to hear the words of the Lord.”.
8.12: And they will wander from sea to sea and from north to east, they will go back and forth to seek the word of the Lord, but they will not find it.
8.13: In those days the beautiful virgins and young men will be exhausted by thirst.
8.14: Those who swear by the sin of Samaria and say, "As surely as your God lives, O Dan, by the way of Beersheba," will fall and not rise again.

9.1: I saw the Lord standing by the altar and he said, «Strike the capital so that the thresholds shake and shatter them on the heads of all. And those who remain I will slaughter with the sword; not one will be saved, not one will escape.”.
9.2: If they enter Sheol, my hand will bring them out; if they ascend to heaven, I will bring them down.
9.3: If they hide on the summit of Carmel, I will seek them out and take them there, and if they hide from my sight at the bottom of the sea, there I will command the serpent to bite them.
9.4: And if they go into captivity before their enemies, there I will command the sword to slaughter them. And I will fix my eye on them, for harm and not for good.»
9.5: The Lord, the God of hosts, touches the earth and it melts and all its inhabitants mourn; it rises entirely like the Nile and sinks like the river of Egypt.
9.6: He builds his steps in the heavens and establishes his vault on the earth; he calls for the waters of the sea and pours them out over the face of the earth: the Lord is his name.
9.7: Are you not to me like the sons of the Cushites, children of Israel, declares the Lord? Did I not bring Israel up from the land of Egypt, and the Philistines from Caphtor, and the Arameans from Kir?
9.8: Behold, the eyes of the Lord God are fixed upon the sinful kingdom, and I will destroy it from the face of the earth, yet I will not utterly destroy the house of Jacob, declares the Lord.
9.9: For behold, I will give orders and shake the house of Israel among all the nations, as wheat is shaken with a sieve, and the good grain will not fall to the ground.
9.10: All the sinners of my people will perish by the sword, they who say, «Disaster will not come near us, disaster will not overtake us.»
9.11: On that day I will raise up the fallen tabernacle of David, I will repair its breaches, I will restore its ruins, and I will rebuild it as in the days of old.,
9.12: so that they may possess the remnant of Edom and all the nations upon which my name has been invoked, declares the Lord, who does these things.
9.13: “The days are coming,” declares the Lord, “and the plowman will join the reaper, and the treader of the grapes will join the sower of the seed; the mountains will drip with new wine, and all the hills will flow with it.”.
9.14: I will bring back the captives of my people Israel, they will rebuild the ruined cities and inhabit them, they will plant vineyards and drink their wine, they will make gardens and eat their fruit.
9.15: And I will plant them in their own land, and they will never again be uprooted from their land, which I have given them, says the Lord your God.
